content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(3, 7, 0), (4, 5, 1), (7, 8, 4), (0, 3, 10), (4, 6, 10), (3, 6, 13), (2, 8, 14), (3, 9, 14), (0, 4, 17), (2, 4, 21), (1, 6, 24), (0, 7, 25), (5, 6, 26), (2, 6, 28)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 5 exactly at time 1.

We scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node:

* Edge (3, 7, 0) has node 3 and time 0. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (4, 5, 1) has node 4 and time 1. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (7, 8, 4) has node 7 and time 4. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (0, 3, 10) has node 0 and time 10. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 6, 10) has node 4 and time 10. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (3, 6, 13) has node 3 and time 13. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (2, 8, 14) has node 2 and time 14. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (3, 9, 14) has node 3 and time 14. -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 4, 17) has node 0 and time 17. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (2, 4, 21) has node 2 and time 21. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (1, 6, 24) has node 1 and time 24. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (0, 7, 25) has node 0 and time 25. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (5, 6, 26) has node 5 and time 26. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (2, 6, 28) has node 2 and time 28. -> Add node 6.

The nodes matched are 4, 5, 6, and 7.

Answer: [4, 5, 6, 7]
